textByHash.02e1e62cb4b156856006ceb3f68e23df16f34edf: Now thankfully, as horrible as all this is, I believe this is a way for us to break Zemouregal's control over Arrav. To do so, we must recover Arrav's heart.
textByHash.0634e01f628cb34725a557ece8fd1b722e6291ba: You're wondering how I managed to do it?
textByHash.0b14d4c700879a2193049f3e063a211046f3fd03: You're back. Have you managed to make any progress?
textByHash.10263633b2fd40c92b8cdeb02f5b78c26a449141: How kind of you to offer. I definitely wouldn't complain about some extra support, if you're sure you're interested?
textByHash.1054235c86440475cbfdd9af8396cbe50d5cb405: Indeed I did. And I reckon it might also contain the knowledge we need to help old Arrav out.
textByHash.105f75ca32523ce584ad9b5f68b4257e5509de36: I'm afraid that's not all, Captain. has made some further discoveries.
textByHash.111e379dd6a517435aa97e0e9bcd73d6232835fc: Let's see what we can find down that trapdoor.
textByHash.1217d882624050d059ef0918842df58593463ac3: If we are to recover the heart, we will need a way to not only keep it preserved, but to keep it beating as well.
textByHash.12bf08f8b4cbdd4a4c8bcd0ff5453c6b9a18eecf: Arrav's heroic deeds continued, until he was granted a vision of the future. He saw the Mahjarrat Zemouregal claiming Avarrocka as his own.
textByHash.16548d74109c7b2fc9cbeb73c428365befe152cf: Not quite, but I have the next best thing. One of my contacts has been supporting my research by investigating Mahjarrat activity in the far north.
textByHash.17aafc95044e47cda611ca769f72f9e51a185404: I will take the heart and begin working right away. Given that Zemouregal knows we have it, I intend to go to ground for this one.
textByHash.1a2222b690f6de357c00871f048a896d2f0c7469: I'm ready when you are. Once you head in, I'll arrange a distraction to lure some of those zombies away. I'll meet you back here once you've recovered the heart. Good luck.
textByHash.1cdf0cf6de6cf21a66b99c3a2f5b6339ccbcb5d1: Well that's just all the more reason for me to be here. If Zemouregal is planning a new attack on Varrock, an expert on his race might come in quite useful.
textByHash.21718724d5e845535084622ab44cb75357d8931d: It is, and I apologise if you feel I have deceived you. My past with the Mahjarrat, and especially with Zemouregal is... complicated. Again, I would prefer we not discuss this further.
textByHash.21c18c311bac1ca8e96465b0957bc272f9d2503b: Now, I still have quite a bit of preparatory work to do before we consider heading inside, but once that's done, I'll be in touch.
textByHash.2368349c8f97fc52c7ae14e1a6ac9327d5bec7be: That's one way to put it. Good job I was keeping an eye on things.
textByHash.241d20dd2e92286089d3f38a69c611efff456f3d: Ah, I heard about that. Excellent work! You may well know then that the former owner of that shield, Arrav himself, once defended Varrock against a zombie invasion led by a Mahjarrat named Zemouregal.
textByHash.243f44aeb5f58774a0ba4a8d182a225be0fe84bc: Ah, hello there. Good to see you.
textByHash.260cc403917e28dec4169aa96adf2b43ce4990b0: Determined to stop Zemouregal in any way he could, Arrav set out to find a powerful weapon with which to best him. His journey took him to the Imcando Dwarves, who granted him a powerful shield.
textByHash.279b6f66c2bc2ae546cbd11c27545454ce62af88: Oh, hello there. You look familiar. Have we met before?
textByHash.279ecd5d7ea7307fd3a16960ac02824e3f57536c: A trapdoor! Now what do you reckon the chances are that we can unlock it with that key?
textByHash.2e097ab579ce7e05c6125196937c270f83ee4a09: Not at all. I've done all sorts of things in my time. In fact, at one point I was an adventurer like you!
textByHash.2faebc1505c1bf05eb70d724261f974f772bb31f: Zemouregal must have a way of doing this, but I have a feeling he won't be willing to share the details on how.
textByHash.2fbff44c148ff575061d48f5198c1bfbea490edd: We need to try and follow the trail left by those zombies.
textByHash.302db47d8dec95c8198085b0ab81cd024fe4c061: Ah, I've lived in many places over the years, but I wouldn't really say I have a home. I guess Varrock would be the closest answer.
textByHash.32643171a813ec878dadac3cfe2c21011fc97711: How kind of you to offer. I'm not in need of any extra help right now though.
textByHash.32b1ddd23d869498f30ec7bc58cb00ebf7f670b7: Very well. I'll be here when you're ready.
textByHash.33dd058b16d5c96bcfaf20d0c18462c551a0abe5: Now, provided my distraction works, you should find the area on the other side of the sewers free of zombies. That will give you the time and space needed to get that vault door open.
textByHash.34a4041541d6930a4ba6903ff92640ec67c6621c: A key? I wonder what it opens... Best we keep a hold of it and find out.
textByHash.3523a23675ccca7ebc68d9d71b9a00efb13e7bed: Ready to get back to it? My hope is we can pick up the trail left by those zombies right outside the inn.
textByHash.36ac2385b1188e96b9123639176638769ae61d42: If the alarm is sounded, a gas will be released, incapacitating all living things in the area.
textByHash.389f85f8faff8fb2a8c4847023faaf9aecd2fa4a: Very well. If you change your mind, I'll be here.
textByHash.39da80b7f1ece5bf1f0b1192f7382cc69a6c9eaf: On the other side of the spells the vault door is found. This door is guarded by zombies. If they see an intruder, they will set off the alarm.
textByHash.3a27d2b18b7e09f6d0aa145d786e302546ce9f2b: Ah, there you are. Ready to perform a heist? Remember, you'll need some sort of insulated protection along with a crossbow and grappling hook.
textByHash.3a8bbfe32d726f42634de97338a4adc8132c0812: The eternal quest for knowledge, of course! The name's Elias White. I'm something of an explorer.
textByHash.3d940d7e651062b759dd13b478ae3e41279279b5: Ready to get back to it? We need to find out what's hiding down that trapdoor.
textByHash.3f18c2a214372e8a1f15b518ca5abcaa65bbecca: And on that note, how do you feel about a bit of tomb raiding?
textByHash.3f55036351d48324862fcc45463f25e3ae9d1247: Almost certainly. The end result of the process is an undead slave, bound to whoever possess their still-beating heart.
textByHash.417669d7a418438c83196759a1ac3767b7d2ba70: I'd say it's safe to assume that there are some sort of defensive measures in place to deter any would-be grave robbers.
textByHash.428b84c9a9548622b22746405b07638a4677fb4e: He recently spotted Zemouregal entering an old fort near the mountain of Trollweiss.
textByHash.434cbc6cf60c5acbb955df0254814b15a8824321: Hundreds of years have now passed, and while there are few who haven't heard of Arrav, many have forgotten Zemouregal and his promise.
textByHash.448d0de3087f858f8a573f541f77868fc133feef: It seems that the inner section of Zemouregal's Base has state of the art security. The corridor into the area is filled to the brim with human detection spells. These will set off an alarm.
textByHash.46245bc65ccb4b803230db2018e591f36c80bf05: I hope you will not judge me too harshly for that. We are all entitled to our secrets, and even now I would prefer not to share them.
textByHash.48360a9c0f5e82bd4e67ec8686a5a1863e1ffb37: You'll find the entrance atop Trollweiss Mountain. I'd suggest you take a pickaxe with you. My contact mentioned that there had been a few cave-ins.
textByHash.4c4056a1827b44e01a8743fc9c59132af19293e9: First of all, we're going to need a distraction to lure some of those zombies out. I have no doubt that I'll be able to act as suitable bait. While I do that, you'll need to get through those sewers.
textByHash.4ec84f434836c6d3cd3ab924359f6c7095be0b84: Not quite here, but up on the plateau. There's an old mastaba up there that I believe dates from a time when the race resided in these lands.
textByHash.50168d10115f26d1eb3d5ad12f4f5705eb102ddf: Ah, there you are. How did things go after I left you?
textByHash.5280506497f0e3dfd601f4bd80466ef36fd67288: However, reaching it is going to pose some significant problems...
textByHash.52e571080b60c2cc3b7a14a5e1792303bf2196f1: We may not have been able to save Arrav today, but thanks to your discoveries, we now have a lead to follow. It will take me some time, but once I have something on this heart business, I'll be in touch.
textByHash.53d7b3145f5254b129f0d9d2000bc5f6c3fbe120: The victim must do whatever they are commanded, but unlike most other forms of necromancy, their mind, and indeed their very soul, remains intact. It all perfectly matches what you encountered with Arrav.
textByHash.552b983a24aaeceaf45532b4d7adaff53486bd03: A generous offer! I still have quite a bit of preparatory work to do before I dare head inside, but once that's done, maybe we can work something out.
textByHash.58bfd69fe969c7ac6ad33a2b97aa9f14d39f0717: And on that note, I intend to get started right away. Until we meet again.
textByHash.5b9f06c6c30d776b1d9612c3362ffa17cbf72b1e: Ah, of course. You offered to help with my work, didn't you? I'm still very keen to take you up on that offer at some point, but a more pressing matter has delayed that somewhat.
textByHash.5ba8707fa454734b7ff82c4aa07ca923451f72a2: Try giving the shield to them. Whoever the right person is, the shield should respond to them in some way.
textByHash.5df2e6812af9e7269c21f4a7ca265353714f2845: Well, keep at it. Once you have some information on how we can keep Arrav's heart preserved, meet me back here.
textByHash.5ee5eafbf777998ef6991278c762f4517d3491b4: A zombie with his mind intact... Most zombies are mere husks, their souls having long departed their bodies. For one to still be sentient is highly unusual. Could it mean...
textByHash.5f8ef7afa7b8fb74f629447345fbb86aae1b24a9: I have no doubt you can. While you work on that, I will get started on our next issue: Finding where Zemouregal is keeping Arrav's heart. Hopefully I'll have something by the time you're done.
textByHash.6075051ab82aca6cc825057b895810bb21ca0828: Doing so will require further research, not to mention the right opportunity.
textByHash.607cdca7578a67b5e1b58a3b1b1b5949b60ad33f: Arrav. So the stories are true. The legendary hero of Avarrocka, now the slave of a disgusting necromancer.
textByHash.615a45af844b66f4a7586151d95147d582a679fe: Varrock has an interesting history when it comes to zombies. Tell me, what do you know of Arrav?
textByHash.63e202c3bd5094fed7183a8fcd0a591a3cbd789a: Yes. My contact located some tunnels that looked like they might lead right into the fort. Seems like a far better option than the front door.
textByHash.66678aa41f4267c33c930995e79ab03966292471: Stay safe out there. Until we meet again.
textByHash.668f0a8a9b2d88d86490f2ec724e1d3b35c8a7e6: You might not hear from me for some time, but fear not, I will be in touch again once things are ready.
textByHash.689195f23ab58c75ae9c8872d8783193163a9198: Back in the Fourth Age, a group of travellers came upon an abandoned child. It is said the child was unusual for one so young. Dark eyes and a fierce countenance, yet fair hair and skin, with a kind smile.
textByHash.6c86b33ac0531a84a3144c2c12c2135a1b61e3bb: The key difference: The process is performed while the victim is still alive!
textByHash.6fd41ebb03763459139c02649928ecc6df634d2c: It does appear to pose somewhat of a challenge, but if we engage in a bit of cunning, it may not be as impossible as you might think.
textByHash.6fe03e7cf7a9232cf237811da1c35b53b036abdf: The vault door is made of five-inch thick runite-enforced steel, with a four digit access code. When the code is entered incorrectly more than twice, the alarm will be set off.
textByHash.70ada3dfdb3e84c86bd6038703ffd8d4133dee74: Now, are you ready to perform a heist? Remember, you'll need some sort of insulated protection along with a crossbow and grappling hook.
textByHash.73f8b8568346446e839a9054bdd3203729d1ee9c: The travellers saw this as an omen and set up camp where they found him. The camp soon grew into the town of Avarrocka, or Varrock as we now call it.
textByHash.74b83bce9193f7fa0ff84b2dbb6af0b921949cc6: Yes, there's a chamber here marked as containing a heart! It must be Arrav's!
textByHash.74d0e96d97949837a18f3eaafdf66493509a4432: Well, without getting bogged down in all the details, my research led me to some information on a rare and unusual form of necromancy that was once practiced in these lands.
textByHash.77baeebe59e8c300670ad345561ae67fdfdc9977: Of course. As I said, my search is for knowledge.
textByHash.77ebfcda2c7ba47de50376bd25c8ab58538ae59c: Excellent stuff! So how do we prepare this jar?
textByHash.795b1ff80fd41401c0e8a1f528cf8014a5475ebe: The situation isn't great, but all may not be lost. Did you manage to find out anything about how the shield works?
textByHash.79bc31746260256aae7bff92a0a4a6e9d506a1ad: Indeed. I recived an interesting yet concerning tip-off from a contact of mine regarding some of my research. I came here to investigate for myself.
textByHash.7a3881c9c65600fc6e070dc1c14633fdc1a9ee1d: Ah, hello there. Good to see you. I take it you recived my message?
textByHash.7bee6c411ad5940c53afde74d2079cdd09dbbeca: You'd better head over to Camdozaal. Time is not on our side.
textByHash.7e2a00f2332346e2058d079e29aff270684e4cd6: The events at Varrock showed that too many have forgotten the dangers of the Mahjarrat.
textByHash.81d85ff4c406e289b84c9f21e5aa1d1ab71f64d8: Now, once you head in, I'll arrange a distraction to lure some of those zombies away. I'll meet you back here once you've recovered the heart. Good luck.
textByHash.833de90831dfd85e4998685ed5811c364b73e6e6: Apparently the zombies were seen forming ranks. Zombies don't naturally organise themselves, so someone must be organising them. I can think of worse candidates than Zemouregal.
textByHash.851add53f2c38b3ae6868b35c567222042065e1d: Indeed. I've managed to make a few friends in my time.
textByHash.8a410ca03797e9bfb0bc669081bef916a569b0c8: I'll meet you back at the Jolly Boar Inn.
textByHash.8c94152a57497f196db693e4fc3a063d520103e9: These sewers could be used to bypass the spells. However, surges of energy are periodically sent through the water there.
textByHash.91afb0665ce8ac5ffcfd5ae80f6ec2c53b667db2: Good luck in there. I will meet you here once you are done.
textByHash.92a8a181126a63e2fad53efd03ddb28cae2fbbe5: Very well. I'll meet you in the palace once you're done. Be careful.
textByHash.92b9a206f47641cfa935d34cba7e5bf3f837127b: Indeed. Turns out the desert was the best place to begin my research into Arrav and his heart. Not too surprising of course, given the burial practices here.
textByHash.94b078156cd77d9802f9ff1692ba9f101a2a4707: But now, they've all but vanished! Most will go through their whole lives without ever seeing one, and their most common appearance these days is in the spooky stories parents use to keep their children in line.
textByHash.9624d8730328b79cb6f8511139a16d3d645e0682: Oh, nothing to worry about now. Still, we may be able to work with this. What did Arrav have to say to you?
textByHash.9994134abb381f1a18f33412e7b2e02ff890091e: Excellent. Once you head in, I'll arrange a distraction to lure some of those zombies away. I'll meet you back here once you've recovered the heart. Good luck.
textByHash.9a61cab22cdbb8da2c8fa926cd78b66433df1d44: Very well. I'll meet you back at the Jolly Boar Inn.
textByHash.9c60277b4252ddc1350a3bedd0355c685b5e68d8: Thankfully not. I just found that when it came to my work, it was the history I discovered that interested me more than the treasure.
textByHash.9d6b117a78b7b2743347670757ce3ed6440f2ec8: Now, there is a sewage system running under some parts of the base that can be accessed from the nearby kitchen.
textByHash.9eb838158c1642221b2c4c7b048c2a6b52e38cd9: In the long term, potentially. But this will require a fair amount of research. Something I fear we have little time for right now.
textByHash.9f939622e1505cfbdff3028a2487415da0141dcb: Arrav sacrificed himself to save his city, but Zemouregal survived.
textByHash.9fe4a0297696f6ffc0c74ff4dbe91fd9a5fa1696: The Mahjarrat. It's fair to say they are quite the subject of fascination for me. Many of them once held dominion over huge parts of the world. Zamorak himself was a Mahjarrat, and he managed to achieve godhood!
textByHash.a01e3fe62a6208ca2aa1a0d5fbd36f7f906da5d4: Hello again. Have you managed to explore those tunnels yet?
textByHash.a10b7ef55ca41253984b235054e536bfc5f237bc: The key element of the process involved the removal and preservation of the heart, much in the same way that the priests of Icthlarin do so during their burial ceremonies.
textByHash.a249a257ef69892724da73b0ee3b546ac2c1ce5e: Finally, at the back of the vault, we have the pedestal holding Arrav's heart.
textByHash.a45ba48b049817f885b0a6f5f887f40b756f96cb: Well it turns out the desert was the best place to begin my research into Arrav and his heart. Not too surprising of course, given the burial practices here.
textByHash.a4d17197e8472fa6155d1bab887ac90517b436db: The entrance is right on the top of Trollweiss. I'd suggest you take a pickaxe with you. My contact mentioned that there had been a few cave-ins.
textByHash.a6267ee2eb1bb6a63356343a456b2d7cc2b32c34: Hello again. Did you manage to find a way into that fort?
textByHash.a641ec7632363e4466985ba23db97de241bc950f: Well let's not waste too much time. We need to see what's down there.
textByHash.a7ad63a6c79e68ee60b92dadc40d3f0922d95661: Understandable. I would suggest you invest in some kind of insulation to protect yourself from the surges.
textByHash.aaa54b21fff6ea1614af187113420e84fff15f69: Hello again. I found this earlier. I imagine you'll be needing it.
textByHash.ac4c409e404379b9f50d66c4c39ec183d285682e: Pretty well. In fact, I think I'm getting close to something. It's related to that old mastaba up on the plateau. I reckon it might contain the knowledge we need to help old Arrav out.
textByHash.ae0d058aa2a0cb71e817e30bfd4130e18704a1ed: Really? It hadn't struck me as a tourist area, what with it being a bit troll-ridden. Anyhow, you shouldn't need to be doing any tobogganing this time. Instead, you'll be heading underground.
textByHash.af041e2bfcb6275ee7d4e63d0874dd7cb5502d76: Indeed. An actual Mahjarrat, and a very dangerous one at that. Ancient tomes can only teach us so much. If I want to learn everything there is to know about their race, it's going to take some more hands on work.
textByHash.b0b917765a654778692b275443a95c130c4102d1: I was assisting some archologists in the south when I found an old tablet describing of a race of seemingly immortal shapeshifters wielding the power of gods. Naturally, I was immediately fascinated!
textByHash.b2e3bc56876be53378736a960026dbe276684aac: Exactly. With the shield in hand, Arrav returned home and faced off against Zemouregal and his zombie horde. Thanks to him, the invasion was repelled, but at the cost of Arrav's life.
textByHash.b3a0951630674fd0df294bc1d1b66a746434415c: Zombies are not particularly light on their feet, so my hope is we can pick up their trail. The sightings were apparently right outside the inn.
textByHash.b487d598741153c8cd5baac626f3e906239cab57: We need to try and pick up the trail left by those zombies.
textByHash.b56dd4708c91c7f4b4a9458dc7d3ec58279975ef: His heart? Interesting... Could that be how Zemouregal is controlling him?
textByHash.b7ae6feaccc53cc6fe05293673c48468fa0596a2: Oh yes! If nothing else, I've definitely lived a varied life.
textByHash.b89812cd57519afdca5527affb931440255da1d9: My hope is the mastaba contains the answers we need. It was built to house a Pharaoh who ruled the desert thousands of years ago.
textByHash.b93e8522dc05008aa7d83611df277934688dfeb7: I have never kept it secret from you that I have a history with the Mahjarrat, although I may have chosen not to divulge some of the more... specific details.
textByHash.b9e8ea6a64a4f6752406888f51c7a798d9809b43: Well, let me know once it's ready and we can discuss our next steps.
textByHash.ba81acf2875eaffdd7d9fa30fd2cf5eb5e0b7801: Then let's look around and find out!
textByHash.bd2ae0c17b53c5b93c8ba020d4f2a8a32f909819: You know, it's been so long that I can barely remember. As I recall, I first found mention of their kind right here in the desert.
textByHash.bf58c802b9c41f62bb21d3aa9b21ddd07db41785: Very well. I will wait for you here.
textByHash.c01f1d54c0c767f1311ca025edac24329f73c420: Look! Footprints. Let's see where they lead us.
textByHash.c264e365e3ffce2755e532a08a240f738a0138af: Oh, not at all. Given recent events, Zemouregal is of particular interest of course, but Mahjarrat activity in general has been increasing recently, especially up north.
textByHash.c2d90e9d4c399f3395f47c19944f9ac8266ddb07: Well thanks to our good friend here, we have the very shield that was used to stop Zemouregal the first time he attacked.
textByHash.c328f94cc361b6f08462a41f4b14a732ab9053b5: Well, when you're ready, you know where to find me.
textByHash.c5aa6a518d0a1b460b464e5657cc9c1c0798daf8: Then it's just as I feared. I've already reported our discovery to Captain Rovin. Come, let's fill him in on everything else you've learnt.
textByHash.c8238d3eea976a31175189ec30044f489649ee80: However, I received an interesting yet concerning tip- off from a contact of mine regarding some of my research. I came here to investigate for myself.
textByHash.c91d2d480aeeb643f4bd2cbfae5abda75372d137: Indeed! From the frozen wastes of the north to the ancient tombs of the desert, I've seen it all.
textByHash.cbd6b2552bbd701dab1c9f6f7dad702ce2a5c76b: True. Perhaps it would be wise for us to split up.
textByHash.cc0a43440d52914b34d2aa0391e2ef4f9e816601: Of course, I soon went on to find out that they are not only fascinating, but also very dangerous. I must admit that I see it as something of a duty to keep my research up to date.
textByHash.cf68ed2c48a540f03cf6ad763f31849f9714964a: Let's see if we can work out what this dungeon is for.
textByHash.d0254f0f436d0d6dffe6b42af1463696964e6485: She was known for many feats, with one of them being her work in saving the victims of a Mahjarrat necromancer.
textByHash.d0e0e14fead98a876ab68df90a9a525539e9f7da: Excellent stuff! I had no doubt you'd discover what we needed. You'll be pleased to hear that I've managed to make some good progress as well.
textByHash.d2d3e2cfcbe1fb52528ff5b15b6f12820aee83eb: Sounds good. Let me know once it's ready and we can discuss our next steps.
textByHash.d3b1cd0b2087f7ba6735900df3c972fbb8c858ea: Ah, hello again. How goes the tomb raiding?
textByHash.d4ee406dff4f4aa659f86e524a4b952a2cad2af6: Now, why don't we instead focus this conversation on something far more positive than my history. We have successfully recovered Arrav's heart!
textByHash.d5c3dc63792a9244b474fe336c657fb982fc6ff2: Zemouregal has not been seen since, but it is said that his last act before retreating was to claim Arrav's body, promising to turn him into an undead slave to one day return with.
textByHash.d5d3dc73833f26992a047357e2800c52f44421ad: To answer that, our best bet would likely be to seek out the creators of the shield: The Imcando Dwarves of Camdozaal.
textByHash.d7f8700015215e8d4e070a3caf5b7df54ec97e50: I do indeed. Don't worry, I'll look after it until we need it.
textByHash.df9f84efb777f2e30bd478ae68aff962a4f049f9: You need to head into that mastaba up on the plateau and see if you can find some information on how we can keep Arrav's heart preserved. Once you have something, meet me back here.
textByHash.e4d07c4808deabed07d8f0f5cb5863cdaeeff878: Precisely! Gather those items and then meet me outside Zemouregal's Base near Varrock. It's time to recover a heart!
textByHash.e52e200d9d3f8a7704c7a92cf71953df6cdf343c: As for the child, he grew to become a mighty hero. After defending the town from a goblin attack, he took on the name Arrav, a curse word in goblin tongue, for he had become the Curse of Goblins.
textByHash.e89c1bd708f12ad7cfc2424767aa12180d5674a1: Not exactly. In fact, I was previously hunting down information on an ancient race deep within the desert.
textByHash.e95bd0038d23da5b781faf913d94bfca3ea4c50d: Oh, and a word of warning. When I was asking around about the mastaba, I was told about a group of... well they apparently called themselves explorers. They entered the mastaba, but never came back out.
textByHash.ea7492a1ddf581ba6a6f3fa13eb861e6a45bf6e5: It's nothing much, but apparently one of the patrons in here was ranting and raving about zombies. Most just dismissed him as having had one too many, but I'm not so sure.
textByHash.ea96bf0be417dda4ed9e874ab670b724d5155d10: Well, it turns out the desert was the best place to begin my research into Arrav and his heart. Not too surprising of course, given the burial practices here.
textByHash.ef8e8dade49fefdaa781b8eba2bd5ce78add6166: Inside the vault there is a criss-cross of special fire ray enchantments that Zemouregal has made. Touching any of these will do significant damage and set off the alarm.
textByHash.f04009747d720c829779f9f68d411a6b1f611f42: Why are you trying to destroy that? We need it. I can look after it for you if you want?
textByHash.f060e5abe146d3f3e0742d092bdd1330d017f4cd: Indeed. I have encountered many forms of dark magic in my time, but I must confess, few have filled me with the same anger that I felt upon discovering this vile form of necromancy.
textByHash.f07ec1536a1168f1ae785b05d653954a16fc57cd: Have you managed to make any progress?
textByHash.f8efef5123329ce7a27ed38e42ed9a41709f83a0: In fact, as you might recall, I was here before to look into that old mastaba up on the plateau.
textByHash.f910a3642114940eea54107748d68eb8571e6d17: It's quite the tale and I doubt I'd be able to do it justice, but I can try and give you an overview.
textByHash.f9357816c0dfe6e398d84da61c7af754d876dd0f: I see it's just as I suspected. Zemouregal is the one responsible for our zombie gatherings. We should inform the authorities in Varrock at once.
textByHash.f97efe897f4b394d58256a19c82a373ed0b1bf3c: Eventually, yes, but while the heart is key to Zemouregal's control over Arrav, merely possessing it won't be enough to break that control.
textByHash.fac3c04557e51f5875abc1ef4d58a1df45284a94: Information lost to the ages. Specifically, information on an ancient race.
textByHash.fb47c4ab2788a0cef0ee6114010623b3c683c7f5: Well, no great heist is complete without the use of a crossbow and a grappling hook.
textByHash.ffe6fc3deb53c3df95c8c816053cafad362ab4dc: I've lived in many places over the years, but I wouldn't really say I have a home. I guess here in Varrock would be the closest answer.
